diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with primary amenorrhea, sensorineural deafness, marfanoid features, and a normal karyotype (46 XX). Laboratory findings showed low levels of estrogen, testosterone, and progesterone, along with elevated levels of gonadotropins (FSH and LH). Audiometric evidence confirmed mild bilateral sensorineural deafness, leading to the diagnosis of Perrault syndrome.
